full_text_rationale: Full text introduces a long-horizon coding-agent benchmark for emergent specification tracking and evaluates ProjectGuard, an external project-state layer that persists semantic design commitments and structural codebase state across coding turns and proactive restarts, with quantitative Claude Code and Codex results.
extractor_notes: Fresh search architecture extraction batch 013; controlled-comparison and quality status remain pending aggregate recomputation.
autores: Lu Yan, Xuan Chen, Xiangyu Zhang
titulo: When the Specification Emerges: Benchmarking Faithfulness Loss in Long-Horizon Coding Agents
formato_armazenamento: ProjectGuard stores semantic project state with revisable design commitments and structural project state with files, classes, functions, and interfaces, then uses compatibility-aware forecasting and proactive restart decisions across coding turns.
framework_agente: ProjectGuard external project-state layer evaluated within the SLUMP emergent-specification benchmark for Claude Code and Codex
llm_utilizado: Claude Code and Codex coding platforms
metodo_avaliacao: benchmark; emergent-versus-single-shot comparison; mitigation case study; platform comparison
benchmarks_utilizados: SLUMP benchmark built from 20 ML papers, 371 components, and approximately 60 coding requests per paper
metricas_desempenho: Mean Component Faithfulness, DIR, IF50, fully faithful component counts, severe failures, compaction/restart behavior, state size
metricas_cl: External persistent semantic and structural project state across long-horizon coding turns, compatibility forecasts, and proactive restarts.
baseline_comparado: Emergent specification runs, single-shot specification control, and ProjectGuard mitigation on Claude Code/Codex
tamanho_memoria_ao_longo_tempo: Mean external state size is reported as 390 KB for Claude Code and 286 KB for Codex in ProjectGuard runs.
custo_por_tarefa: NR monetary cost; external state size and compaction/restart behavior are reported.
resultado_principal: Under emergent specification, Claude Code drops from 3.031 MCF single-shot to 2.718 emergent and Codex drops from 3.245 to 3.242; ProjectGuard on Claude raises MCF from 2.718 to 3.000, recovers 90% of the gap, increases fully faithful components from 118 to 181, and reduces severe failures from 72 to 49.
desempenho_vs_baseline: Single-shot outperforms emergent specification on 16 of 20 papers for Claude Code and 14 of 20 for Codex. ProjectGuard improves Claude IF50 on 15 of 20 papers; Codex semantic gains are small but DIR improves on 15 of 19 analyzed cases.
custo_vs_baseline: ProjectGuard adds external state averaging hundreds of KB and changes compaction/restart behavior; no monetary or token-cost comparison is reported.
limitacoes: ProjectGuard is evaluated as a mitigation case study for emergent specification, not as a complete standalone coding-agent architecture across many software-engineering domains.
ameacas_validade: Component scoring rubric, paper selection, emergent request scripts, Claude Code/Codex platform behavior, hidden artifact design, and ProjectGuard implementation choices may affect validity.
